data.title: Prototype Roadmap — Base System to First Testable Version
data.scope: Base prototype on arithmetic expressions and simple linear equations to first measurable test result. Excludes: reasoned library, second meta-level system, weighted graphs, hyperedges (unless Phase 6 forces it), full self-evolving rewrite system.

phases.title: Graph Substrate and Operations
phases.notes: PerspectiveGraph, operations module, 9 passing tests. restore() method added to PerspectiveGraph for clean revert encapsulation.
phases.description: Implement directed graph structure with binary edge typing (structural / operational). Implement the Graph Operation Interface: match, apply, revert, complexity, lookup, register. Unit test each operation group on hand-constructed graphs. No layer structure yet — flat graphs only.
phases.exit_condition: All five operation groups work correctly on hand-constructed test graphs.

phases.title: Input Layer and Encoding
phases.description: Encode arithmetic expressions and linear equations as directed graphs. Each constant value encoded as a binary tree rooted at its node — no node attributes, identity emerges from structure. Operators and equality as internal nodes. Variable x as anonymous structural node. Input layer immutable. Seed rules provided as bootstrap scaffold — present at generation zero, full GA mutability from generation zero. Seed rule displacement by library-informed rules is the success signal.
phases.exit_condition: Expressions reliably converted to base graphs. Sample manually inspected and encoding confirmed sane.

label_format.arithmetic: Terminal value node only — e.g. 7. No wrapper.
label_format.equations: Solved form graph — e.g. x = 3. Irreducible — x = 3 does not collapse to 3.
label_format.collapse_rule: x = x collapses to x. 3 = 3 collapses to 3. x = 3 does not collapse — both sides are distinct.

complexity_ladder: Level 2: One-step solving — e.g. x + 4 = 7, label: x = 3
Level 3: Two-step solving — e.g. 2x + 4 = 10, label: x = 3
Level 4: Variable on both sides — e.g. 2x + 4 = 3x - 1, label: x = 5

phases.title: Layer Structure and Compression
phases.description: Implement multi-layer structure. Layer identity is representational scale, not operation type. Implement layer fusion and splitting. Implement per-layer rulesets.
phases.exit_condition: Placeholder layer boundaries manually assigned and documented as stubs. Replacement by GA-converged boundaries is the P5b success signal, not a P3 deliverable.

phases.title: Empirical Library
phases.description: Implement rule-invariant pair storage. Implement label-correlation filter. Implement library lookup. Implement recognition cascade.
phases.exit_condition: After exposure to a subset of training expressions, library contains non-trivial entries. Lookup on a new expression returns matches.

phases.title: Minimal GA over Rule Graphs
phases.description: GA on rule graphs. Fitness signal: given a fixed label, minimize variance in subgraph structures produced across different expressions with that label. Library entry is byproduct of convergence. Fixed GA primitives — self-evolution deferred. Start GA from generation zero with seed rules as initial population.
phases.rationale: Load-bearing for empirical library. Without rule improvement, label-conditioned reproducibility is not achievable. Must precede traversal loop.
phases.exit_condition: Rule quality improves across generations. Library entries stabilize. Seed rules begin to be displaced by library-informed rules.
phases.risk: Burn-in cold start — empirical library is empty at training start, seed rules are the only available bootstrap.

phases.title: Traversal Loop
phases.description: Implement traversal loop: apply rules, evaluate compression, update library, repeat. Reversibility enforced. Requires P5b-converged rules to produce meaningful output.
phases.exit_condition: System traverses a batch of training expressions, builds a library, can be queried.
phases.risk: GI cost at rule firing scale — profile before P6.

phases.title: First Test and Decision Gates
phases.description: Run on training subset, evaluate on test subset. Measure accuracy, library size, matching cost. Inspect failure modes for q_07 and q_09 signals. Make hyperedge decision based on evidence.
phases.exit_condition: A number exists and q_07 has a documented empirical answer.

gate.pass: Base directed graph substrate is sufficient. Prototype is valid.
gate.fail: Substrate is insufficient. Hyperedge upgrade path warranted.
gate.fail_note: Fail is not throwaway work. Directed graph is hypergraph at k=2. On fail: GOI, layer hierarchy, library structure, GA mechanics, fitness signal, and seed rules all recycle. Seed rules are valid k=2 hypergraph rules — remain valid starting points when substrate is upgraded. Matching engine and rule representations need extension, not replacement.

known_risks.risk: GI cost at rule firing scale
Encoding design is load-bearing — poor encoding limits discoverability
Burn-in cold start — seed rules are only bootstrap before library accumulates
Unbounded parameter growth in layer hierarchy
